note: Authoritative LFE + Erlang/OTP release timeline. Edit by hand; all lookup tables are derived in-memory by tools/lfesite (see src/releases.rs). Dates are ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). 'approximate' marks dates inferred rather than confirmed (notably pre-1.0 LFE). 'prerelease' marks tagged-but-unpublished releases. Source: docs/lfe-erlang-release-history.md.

lfe.notes: Initial announcement on erlang-questions (Robert Virding); built on Erlang R12B-0. No Lisp shell.
Became a Lisp-2; first LFE shell.
More Common Lisp-inspired forms; lfe_boot, lfe_gen.
Last dev version for R12B-5 and older; future development moves to R13B.
Unicode binary types; lfe_io:format; list* macro.
Era tutorials pin v0.6.1 on OTP R13B04 (LFE Shell V5.7.4).
Travis CI; list comprehensions; match-spec generator.
Function/macro definitions in the shell; block comments.
Maps support era; targets R14+ through OTP 17.
Final 0.x series before 1.0.
First Hex publish of the 1.0 milestone (v1.0/v1.0.1 tagged but not on Hex).
Hex.pm.
Hex.pm.
Long-lived stable tag; active through 2019-2020. Not published to Hex.
First release of the v2.x rewrite cluster after a multi-year gap.
Bug fixes for map handling on older Erlang (Hex date).
First v2.1 release.
Quickfix release (Hex date).
Fixes for comparison macros with 3+ args.
New lc/list-comp and bc/binary-comp implementations in the REPL.
macOS build patch (Hex date).
Fix tests with recent rebar3.
Detection for Mix projects to start the LFE REPL in project context. Most recent Hex.pm publication.
Tagged after 2.2.0; appears in the lfe/lfe README REPL banner. Not yet published to Hex (date approximate).

erlang.notes: Representative date R12B-5; the active R12B series during 2008.
ets improvements, native IPv6, recursive funs, epp macros.
Native OS time, silent process loader, halfword emulator.
Last R-named series before integer versioning.
New integer version scheme. Maps introduced (EEP-43), experimental in 17.0. maps:get/3 added in 17.1.
Maps stabilised. License changed to Apache 2.0. New time API.
Dirty schedulers GA. ETS performance work.
Atom Unicode support. New compiler optimisations.
New logger framework. File I/O moved to NIFs. Unified stack-traces.
Improved binary matching, ssl improvements, distribution improvements.
Improved compiler/SSA optimisations; SSL/TLS hardening; socket NIF.
BeamAsm JIT (x86_64) by default; EEP-48 documentation API.
New shell with completion; AArch64 JIT; incremental Dialyzer.
In-source doc attributes (-doc); maybe on by default; json module.
